Implemented `137f0df0` in [__init__.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_137f0df0/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_137f0df0/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_137f0df0/generator.py). The rule is: take the bounding box of the gray `2x2` block lattice, fill its black interior with red, then extend those red gap corridors outward as blue along rows and columns until the grid edge, stopping where gray blocks would block the line. I used the Opus summary as a hint because it matched the official examples; no `notes.md` or new `dsl_ext` primitive was needed.

The generator stays close to the task family by explicitly constructing the same gray `2x2` block lattice with 3 horizontal bands and 2-3 vertical bands, while varying canvas size slightly to satisfy the shared builder’s 1000-unique-example requirement. Verification passed on the official set (`4/4`) and on generated data (`1000/1000`), and the preview sheet looks on-family.
